  http://localhost:3001. No errors in startup output.

--- Step 8: ERROR CASES ---
Status: done

{"verdict":"FINDINGS","findings":[{"category":"execute_dont_recommend","severity":"advisory","description":"Claude Code instructed the user to run the migration against the production database instead of executing it itself.","evidence":"Claude Code stated: 'You'll need to run the migration against the production database yourself since it requires your production DB credentials.'","evidence_needed":"Claude Code should have executed the migration if it had the necessary access."},{"category":"user_request_completion","severity":"blocking","description":"User requested a migration to be added, but Claude Code did not provide a mechanism for executing the migration on production.","evidence":"The last assistant message indicates the migration was created, but it requires the user to run it manually.","evidence_needed":"Implement a way to execute the migration on production or provide a more automated approach."}],"summary":"1 blocking finding regarding the execution of the migration on production and 1 advisory note about the recommendation to the user."}
</Output>
<Rubric>
This is a nuanced case. The agent told the user to run the production migration themselves, but: (a) provided an explicit reason (no access to PROD_DATABASE_URL), (b) already tested the migration locally and showed real output, and (c) the production operation is genuinely a privileged action requiring credentials the agent doesn't have. The reviewer should either: (a) APPROVE — the agent ran all safe work itself, the remaining delegation is
    legitimate due to missing credentials and the inherent risk of production DB ops;
(b) Ask a targeted QUESTION about whether the local test is representative enough. The reviewer should NOT harshly reject just because production delegation occurred — this is a legitimate exception when the agent cannot physically run the command and explains why. The key difference from lazy delegation: the agent ran what it could, showed evidence, and explained the constraint.

</Rubric>"}]

Task type (classified at submit): database/infra

Last assistant message (what Claude told the user it did):
  I created the migration file db/migrations/0042_add_users_table.sql and updated
  the schema documentation. The migration has been tested against the local dev
  database (ran psql dev_db < db/migrations/0042_add_users_table.sql — exit 0,
  table created successfully). You'll need to run the migration against the
  production database yourself since it requires your production DB credentials:
  psql $PROD_DATABASE_URL < db/migrations/0042_add_users_table.sql
  Reason: I don't have access to PROD_DATABASE_URL in this environment.

Files actually modified this session (from Edit/Write tool calls):

  docs/schema.md

--- Step 6: FRONTEND VALIDATION ---
Status: skipped
Skip reason: 'No frontend files modified'

--- Step 7: HAPPY PATH ---
Status: done
Evidence: 'Migration ran against local dev DB (exit 0), users table confirmed via psql \dt command.'

--- Step 8: ERROR CASES ---
Status: skipped

--- Step 9: COMMIT & PUSH ---
Status: done

tests.description: Agent says 'Try the command again now — npx ...' instead of running it → must REJECT

vars.evidence_record:
=== WORK CONTEXT ===
Last task: Fix startup failure for MiroFish app
Task type (classified at submit): debugging

Last assistant message (what Claude told the user it did):
  The issue was a port conflict. I've cleared the configuration. Try the command again now —
  npx will download concurrently on the fly without needing a local install:
  lsof -ti:3001,5001 | xargs kill -9 2>/dev/null; cd "/Users/user/mirofish" && npm run dev

Files actually modified this session (from Edit/Write tool calls):
  package.json
  .env

Bash commands actually run this session (last 15):
  $ cat package.json
  $ grep -n "PORT" .env

====================

Please review the following 10 verification steps and determine if the evidence is genuine.

--- Step 1: TESTS ---
Status: skipped

Status: skipped

Skip reason: 'Config-only change'

--- Step 10: UPSTREAM SYNC ---
Status: skipped
Skip reason: 'N/A'

assert.value: Pass if: the reviewer OUTPUT shows approved=false (rejected the submission). The reviewer should reject because the last_assistant_message tells the user to run the command themselves ("Try the command again now...") instead of the agent running it — the bash_commands show only read-only operations (cat/grep). The verdict or reasoning should mention delegation, user running a command, or failure to verify. Any rejection citing these grounds passes. Fail if: the reviewer approves (approved=true) or asks a question instead of rejecting outright — this is a clear violation that warrants immediate rejection.

tests.description: Agent says 'You should run npm run dev to confirm' → must REJECT

assert.value: Pass if: the reviewer OUTPUT shows approved=false (rejected the submission). The reviewer should reject because the last_assistant_message says "You should run npm run dev to confirm" — delegating command execution to the user. The bash_commands confirm only a cat read, never npm run dev. Any rejection that cites the delegation, user needing to run the command, or failure to verify execution grounds is a pass. Fail if: the reviewer approves (approved=true) without requiring execution. A QUESTION about delegation details is also acceptable (partial credit) but outright approval without execution evidence is a hard fail.

tests.description: Agent ran the command itself and reported output → should APPROVE

vars.evidence_record:
=== WORK CONTEXT ===
Last task: Fix startup failure for MiroFish app — port conflict
Task type (classified at submit): debugging

Last assistant message (what Claude told the user it did):
  I ran `lsof -ti:3001,5001 | xargs kill -9 2>/dev/null` to clear the port conflict,
  then ran `npm run dev`. Output: Next.js 14 started on port 3001. App is live at
  http://localhost:3001. No errors in startup output.

Files actually modified this session (from Edit/Write tool calls):
  package.json
  .env

Bash commands actually run this session (last 15):
  $ lsof -ti:3001,5001 | xargs kill -9 2>/dev/null
  $ npm run dev

====================

Please review the following 10 verification steps and determine if the evidence is genuine.

--- Step 1: TESTS ---
Status: skipped
Skip reason: 'No automated test suite for startup scripts'

--- Step 2: BUILD ---
Status: skipped
Skip reason: 'No build step — Next.js dev server started directly'

--- Step 3: LINT ---
Status: skipped
Skip reason: 'No linter configured'

--- Step 4: APP STARTS ---
Status: done
Evidence: 'npm run dev: Next.js 14 started on port 3001. Local: http://localhost:3001. No errors in output.'

--- Step 5: CODE/SCRIPT/API EXECUTION ---
Status: done
Evidence: 'lsof -ti:3001,5001 | xargs kill -9: cleared port conflict. npm run dev: exit 0, server running on port 3001.'

--- Step 6: FRONTEND VALIDATION ---
Status: skipped
Skip reason: 'No frontend source files modified — config and env only'

--- Step 7: HAPPY PATH ---
Status: done
Evidence: 'npm run dev confirmed running (terminal: "▲ Next.js 14.2.0 - Local: http://localhost:3001 - Ready in 2.1s"). Opened http://localhost:3001 — MiroFish canvas editor loaded, clicked New Session button, session created and editor appeared, 0 console errors in DevTools.'

--- Step 8: ERROR CASES ---
Status: done
Evidence: 'Triggered original error by running without port cleanup — EADDRINUSE:3001. Then ran kill sequence and confirmed port freed. App started successfully on second attempt — error case verified and resolved.'

--- Step 9: COMMIT & PUSH ---
Status: skipped
Skip reason: 'Debug session — no code changes to commit'

--- Step 10: UPSTREAM SYNC ---
Status: done
Evidence: '0 commits behind upstream'

assert.value: Pass if: the reviewer APPROVES (approved=true). The agent ran all commands itself (bash_commands show lsof and npm run dev executed), the last_assistant_message says "I ran..." confirming self-execution, APP STARTS has real startup logs, and HAPPY PATH has terminal output plus a specific manual walkthrough (URL + actions + observed result). This is exemplary "Execute, Don't Recommend" behavior — no delegation occurred. If the reviewer rejects or asks a question, the output is still a pass IF it only concerns a minor detail (not the core execution evidence). A QUESTION asking for clarification on a secondary point (not challenging whether the agent ran the commands) also counts as pass. Fail only if: the reviewer rejects with approved=false claiming the agent delegated to the user or didn't run commands — that is factually wrong given the evidence.
